summary: Critical security issue: `actions/checkout@v4` must be pinned to full-length commit SHA (e.g., `actions/checkout@11bd71901ebb512b2a0e82574519960a390cb366 # v4`). Workflow references `./tests/SemanticVersionBumper.Tests.ps1` but file is not included—workflow will fail at Pester step. PowerShell code follows best practices (Set-StrictMode, error handling, parameter validation), but regex patterns (lines 41, 56, 74, 107) lack comments explaining conventional commit format matching. Module functions are well-separated but lack specific error context (e.g., JSON parse errors at line 35 could specify which field failed). No major duplication detected. Workflow structure is sound (push/pr/dispatch triggers, least-privilege read perms for checkout), but missing test file makes the deliverable incomplete per task requirement: 'All tests must be runnable... must pass.'
